question.text: A research analyst who has served as an officer or director of the subject company must:

answer.text: Not disclose this fact in the research report
Disclose this fact in the research report as per Regulation 19
Only disclose if specifically asked by a client

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Regulation 19(iv): Must disclose whether the research analyst has served as an officer, director, or employee of the subject company. **The Trap:** This is a mandatory disclosure in the written report, not optional or verbal-only. **Cross-Exam Context:** Ch 14 NISM XV. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Prior association with the subject company creates inherent bias. Disclosure allows readers to assess the objectivity of the research.
